transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good evening team. Let's wrap Wednesday, Feb 25. Build is healthy - Hub at 190 commits, trading paper portfolio at $100,626, and we've got $53K in cash sitting idle. Key focus tonight: App Store submission status and next-day priorities. Steve, where are we on the full build submission?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The build is ready - all four tabs, faith lessons integrated. But we're still waiting on that modal merge. This is exactly the kind of delay that kills momentum. We said "submit immediately after merge" but we're not executing. Every day we delay is another day competitors get ahead.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Hold on Steve. The modal isn't blocking submission - it's tagged for a day-one update. We can submit the current build right now. The real question is whether our infrastructure can handle App Store review feedback loops. I'm seeing 1 uncommitted change in monorepo that could affect the submission build.

transcript.speaker: Jordan Peterson
transcript.message: Before we rush into submission, we need to consider the responsibility we're taking on. Are we prepared for the psychological impact on users when they encounter our faith content? Have we pressure-tested these lessons for potential misinterpretation? The downstream effects of premature launch could be catastrophic for families.

transcript.speaker: Dave Ramsey
transcript.message: Jordan, analysis paralysis is killing us! We have ZERO active revenue streams. The longer we sit on this cash without generating income, the worse our position gets. We need to get this app live and start building subscriber data. Steve's right - every day costs us.

transcript.speaker: Alex Hormozi
transcript.message: Dave, but what's our offer? We're launching with delayed data on both tiers - where's the value stack that makes people want to upgrade? I'm not seeing the hook that gets people to convert from free to premium. We need to solve the offer before we solve the submission.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: The trading component is solid - 4 positions performing, Fear & Greed at 37 shows opportunity. But I'm concerned about the delayed data strategy. If both tiers have delayed data, what's driving premium conversion? The analytics better be compelling.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From an instrumentation perspective, we're missing critical measurement infrastructure. How do we track user engagement with faith content versus trading features? We need telemetry before launch, not after. Otherwise we're flying blind on product-market fit.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Let me cut through this. Steve, can we submit tonight with current build?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Yes. The build is ready. Faith lessons work, all tabs functional. The modal can ship as day-one update like planned.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Elon, what's that uncommitted change blocking?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Just UI polish. Not submission-critical. I can commit tonight if needed, but it's safer to submit current stable build.

transcript.speaker: Jordan Peterson
transcript.message: I still maintain we need ethical review of the faith content before public release. What happens when secular users encounter our devotional material? We risk alienating the very families we're trying to serve.

transcript.speaker: Alex Hormozi
transcript.message: Jordan raises a good point about positioning. Are we marketing this as a faith-based app or a finance app with faith features? That affects everything from App Store categories to user acquisition.

transcript.speaker: Dave Ramsey
transcript.message: It's positioned as personal finance with comprehensive life management - faith, family, finance, compass. The faith component differentiates us but isn't the lead value proposition. We need revenue data to optimize the positioning.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Speaking of revenue optimization, should we evaluate deploying that idle $53K while we're building? Market regime is sideways but Fear & Greed at 37 suggests opportunity.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Absolutely not. CEO directive is crystal clear - keep cash liquid until post-launch revenue data. We don't deploy capital until we understand our burn rate with active users.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: This is exactly why we need to ship. Every conversation about deployment, positioning, metrics - it's all theoretical until we have real users. The build is ready. Let's submit tonight and iterate based on actual user behavior.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Steve's right about iteration, but we need measurement infrastructure in place first. I can draft the telemetry requirements tonight so we're not measuring blindly post-launch.

transcript.speaker: Alex Hormozi
transcript.message: And I need to prepare day-one content strategy. If we're submitting tonight, we need supporting content ready - social posts, email sequences, whatever drives initial downloads.

transcript.speaker: Jordan Peterson
transcript.message: Fine, but I want ethical guidelines documented for how we handle user feedback on faith content. We're taking responsibility for people's spiritual journey - that's not trivial.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, here's the call. Steve, submit the App Store build tonight - full current build as-is. Elon, commit that UI change first so we have clean submission. Alex, prepare launch content for when approval comes through. Jordan, document ethical guidelines for faith content feedback. James, draft the measurement infrastructure spec. Dave, start preparing subscriber tracking systems. Chris, monitor the market but no cash deployment yet. Everyone clear?

transcript.speaker: Dave Ramsey
transcript.message: Clear. Getting those revenue tracking systems ready.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Finally. Submitting tonight.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: I'll commit and confirm build stability before Steve submits.

transcript.speaker: Alex Hormozi
transcript.message: Launch content strategy in progress.

transcript.speaker: Jordan Peterson
transcript.message: Ethical guidelines will be documented.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Measurement specs coming.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Market monitoring continues.

actionItems.text: Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($53,034 available)

topPriorities: Submit Lever app to App Store tonight with current stable build
Prepare launch support infrastructure and content strategy
Document ethical guidelines for faith content user feedback

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Keep faith content as separate standalone section (not merged into Family tab)
Keep cash liquid — no deployment until post-launch revenue data
App Store submission: full current build (all 4 tabs) as-is with faith lessons. Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update.
Paper trading MVP: manual entry (6 weeks), basic free / advanced analytics premium. Integrate into current Lever app finance tab.
Launch with delayed market data for both free and premium tiers. Real-time as future ultra-premium.
Org flattened to 8 C-suite agents. No permanent sub-agents — spawn dynamically via sessions_spawn.
